description_long: This object is a vintage, circular clock with a domed glass face and a metallic body that shows signs of age and wear. The clock features a prominent winding key and a pull cord, both attached to the sides, suggesting it is a mechanical timepiece. The face is cream-colored with dark hour markers and two visible hands, encased in a slightly tarnished metal rim. Small rivets and screws are visible around the perimeter, adding to its industrial aesthetic. The back of the clock has a raised, circular base, likely for stability when placed on a surface. The overall design is robust and utilitarian, with a blend of functional and decorative elements typical of early 20th-century portable clocks.
description: A vintage, round mechanical clock with a domed glass face, metallic body, winding key, and pull cord, featuring a cream dial with dark hour markers and hands.
description_view_0: Front view showing the domed glass face, cream dial, and pull cord extending from the left side.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the winding key and the metallic rim, with the pull cord and its knob visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the raised circular base and the back of the metallic body, with the face partially visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the winding key on the left and the pull cord on the right, with the domed face angled upward.
attribute_face_design: Circular cream face with dark hour markers, two hands, and a domed glass cover; minimal decorative elements.
